CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, text_content TEXT);
INSERT INTO test_table (id, text_content) VALUES (1, 'First entry for testing');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, text_content) VALUES (2, 'Second entry for testing');
SET VT_BATCH_UPDATE ON;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, text_content) VALUES (3, 'Third entry with batch update');
INSERT INTO test_table (id, text_content) VALUES (4, 'Fourth entry with batch update');
SELECT * FROM test_table;
SET VT_BATCH_UPDATE OFF;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, text_content) VALUES (5, 'Fifth entry without batch update');
SELECT * FROM test_table;
SET VT_BATCH_UPDATE ON;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, text_content) SELECT id + 10, CONCAT(text_content, ' (batch)') FROM test_table WHERE id < 3;
SELECT id, text_content FROM test_table WHERE id > 2;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT PRIMARY KEY, related_id INT, content TEXT);
INSERT INTO another_table (id, related_id, content) VALUES (1, 1, 'Related content 1');
INSERT INTO another_table (id, related_id, content) VALUES (2, 2, 'Related content 2');
SET VT_BATCH_UPDATE ON;
INSERT INTO another_table (id, related_id, content) SELECT id + 2, id, CONCAT(content, ' (related)') FROM another_table;
SELECT a.id, a.content, t.text_content FROM another_table a JOIN test_table t ON a.related_id = t.id;
SET VT_BATCH_UPDATE OFF;
Delete FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT * FROM test_table;
CREATE TABLE log_table AS SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE FALSE;
INSERT INTO log_table SELECT * FROM test_table;
SELECT * FROM log_table;
